title: Myerson's Optimal Auction Design Framework
description: A flowchart illustrating the key concepts and logical progression in Myerson's theory for designing revenue-maximizing auctions.
relationships: Private values are transformed by the Virtual Valuation function to determine the 'virtual value'
The allocation rule assigns the item to the bidder with the highest virtual value, if positive
The payment rule is derived from the allocation rule to ensure incentive compatibility (truth-telling is a dominant strategy)
The Revenue Equivalence Principle shows expected revenue is determined by the allocation rule
This framework leads to the design of an Optimal Auction that maximizes the seller's expected revenue
